text: and Pelvic Fascia Preservation Nerve Sparing LARP using new surgical robot Hinotori. This made-in-Japan surgical robot system consists of operation unit, surgical cockpit, and monitor cart. The biggest difference between the DaVinci and Hinotori is that Hinotori arm doesn't grip the trocker. The arm just grips the end of the instrument. Since there is no need to dock the arm and trocker, the area around the trocker is clutter-free. Since there is no remote center, Hinotori can handle cross objects without movement restrictions.

This is the clinical background of the presentation case. MRI indicated that the index tumor was located at the left side transitional zone. We plan to perform the conventional nerve sparing for the cancer side and endoperative fascia preservation nerve sparing for the cancer-free side. On the conventional nerve sparing, the endovalve fascia is cut and prostate lateral side is opened. After the bladder and prostate are divided, the periprostatic tissue is dissected cordially, protecting neurovascular bundles. Meanwhile, on the endoperivic fascia preservation nerve sparing, the bladder and prostate are divided without opening the endoperivic fascia. After that, the anterolateral superficial vascular layer is cut to directly reach the prostate surface and prostate is dissected. The end-pelvic fascia preservation nerve sparing technique enables us to remove the prostate with pelvic muscles completely covered by end-pelvic fascia and vascular layers.
The surgical video shows the left side end-pelvic fascia being opened and dissected cordially as part of the conventional nerve sparing technique.

Using the Hinotori video system, the surgeon preserves the endpelvic fascia on the right side. The detrusor apron is cut, and the bladder and prostate are divided. The urethral catheter is held by the third robotic arm while the posterior side is divided. The retrotrigonal layer is separated, and the bilateral vas deferens and seminal vesicles are grasped.

The surgeon performs conventional nerve sparing on the left side using Hem-o-lok clips to secure the lateral pedicle vessels. The Hinotori robotic arms, with their eight joints, facilitate smooth movements. On the right side, endopelvic fascia preservation nerve sparing is executed, with the anterolateral superficial vascular layer being cut to access the prostate surface. Hem-o-lok clips are applied to prevent bleeding during each incision.

The surgeon dissects the prostate from the posterior side to the right lateral plane, extending cordially to the apex. Electrocautery is avoided to prevent thermal damage to muscles and nerves. At the apex, prostatic muscles are stripped, and the deep dorsal venous complex is ligated and sutured with 3-0 monocryl. The urethra is cut, and the prostate is removed. The left side pelvic muscles are exposed in the prostate bed, while the right side remains covered by the end-pelvic fascia and vascular layers.

On both unilateral and bilateral nerve sparing, there was a tendency that end-pelvic fascia preservation nerve sparing achieves the better urinary continence thirty days after surgery. This is a summary of the pathological result and postoperative clinical course. Thank you for your attention.
